title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 24 - Let's Laminate This SOB!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vHvpM9nSO3Q

---

Daniel (@dungeondive) delivers a quick hobby tutorial on laminating Shadows of Brimstone character sheets — a process he's used for about five years with no issues. His setup is simple: a cheap Swingline hot laminator and 5-mil pouches.

The process is straightforward: slide the character card into the pouch (watching for dust and pet hair attracted by static), feed it through the laminator, then trim with a paper cutter leaving about an eighth of an inch border. The secret weapon is a Kotomaru Pro corner punch with three size settings — Daniel always uses the small — to round off the squared edges for a clean, professional finish.

The practical benefits are clear: laminated sheets survive being passed between players, resist spills and fidgeting, and can be written on with dry-erase markers for tracking stats during play. Daniel runs the trimmed sheets through the laminator a second time to seal the cut edges.

He acknowledges that some players consider modifying original game components sacrilege, but takes a pragmatic view — this is his third complete board game collection, and games are transitory things meant to be used and enjoyed rather than preserved in mint condition.
